---
title: 'How to Make Vanilla Mint Cold Processed Soap'
date: '2024-07-18T01:21:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Sunrise Scentworks'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1186367663746062711'
blogger_filename: '/2024/07/how-to-make-vanilla-mint-cold-processed.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Sunrise Scentworks/feed.atom'
---

Sure, Darrell. Here's a starting point for the blog post:<div><br></div><div>---</div><div><br></div><div>## How to Make Vanilla Mint Cold Processed Soap</div><div><br></div><div>Creating your own handcrafted artisan soap is a rewarding and creative process. Today, we’re going to walk you through making a delightful Vanilla Mint soap using the cold process method. Below, you'll find the ingredients and steps needed to make this nourishing and aromatic soap at home.</div><div><br></div><div>### Ingredients</div><div><br></div><div>- **Saponified Oils of:**</div><div>&nbsp; - Olive</div><div>&nbsp; - Coconut</div><div>&nbsp; - Organic Shea Butter</div><div>&nbsp; - Castor</div><div>&nbsp; - Tallow</div><div>- **Additional Ingredients:**</div><div>&nbsp; - Fragrance (Vanilla Mint)</div><div>&nbsp; - Kaolin Clay</div><div>&nbsp; - Mica</div><div><br></div><div>### Equipment</div><div><br></div><div>- Safety goggles and gloves</div><div>- Digital scale</div><div>- Stainless steel or plastic mixing bowls</div><div>- Stick blender</div><div>- Soap mold</div><div>- Measuring spoons</div><div>- Heat-resistant container for lye solution</div><div>- Thermometer</div><div><br></div><div>### Instructions</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 1: Prepare Your Workspace</div><div><br></div><div>Ensure your workspace is clean and well-ventilated. Wear your safety goggles and gloves to protect yourself from the lye.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 2: Measure the Ingredients</div><div><br></div><div>Using a digital scale, carefully measure the required amounts of each oil (olive, coconut, organic shea butter, castor, and tallow). Set these aside.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 3: Prepare the Lye Solution</div><div><br></div><div>In a heat-resistant container, measure the appropriate amount of water. Slowly add the lye to the water (never the other way around) while stirring gently. This mixture will heat up and release fumes, so make sure you’re in a well-ventilated area. Set aside to cool.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 4: Heat the Oils</div><div><br></div><div>Combine all the measured oils in a large stainless steel or plastic mixing bowl. Heat them gently until they are completely melted and mixed. Allow the oils to cool to a temperature that matches the lye solution (around 100-110°F).</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 5: Combine Lye and Oils</div><div><br></div><div>Once the lye solution and oils are at the correct temperature, slowly pour the lye solution into the oils. Use a stick blender to mix until you reach "trace," a point where the mixture thickens to a pudding-like consistency.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 6: Add Additional Ingredients</div><div><br></div><div>Add your fragrance, kaolin clay, and mica to the soap mixture. Blend thoroughly to ensure even distribution of the additives.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 7: Pour into Mold</div><div><br></div><div>Carefully pour the soap mixture into your prepared mold. Smooth the top with a spatula if necessary.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 8: Insulate and Cure</div><div><br></div><div>Cover the mold with a lid or a piece of cardboard, and wrap it in a towel to insulate. Let the soap sit for 24-48 hours to harden.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 9: Unmold and Cut</div><div><br></div><div>Once the soap has hardened, remove it from the mold. Cut into bars and place them on a drying rack. Allow the soap to cure for 4-6 weeks to ensure all the lye is neutralized and the soap is safe to use.</div><div><br></div><div>#### Step 10: Enjoy Your Soap</div><div><br></div><div>After curing, your Vanilla Mint soap is ready to use and enjoy. Package the bars in a way that showcases their beauty and list the ingredients for your customers.</div><div><br></div><div>### Tips for Success</div><div><br></div><div>- **Temperature Control:** Ensure that both the oils and lye solution are at similar temperatures before mixing.</div><div>- **Safety First:** Always add lye to water, never water to lye, and wear appropriate safety gear.</div><div>- **Customization:** Feel free to experiment with different fragrances, clays, and colorants to create your unique soap bars.</div><div><br></div><div>### Conclusion</div><div><br></div><div>Making cold-processed soap is both a science and an art. With these steps, you can create beautiful, aromatic Vanilla Mint soap at home. Stay tuned for more recipes and soap-making tips!</div><div><br></div><div>---</div><div><br></div><div>Feel free to edit or iterate on this draft as needed!</div>
